skills.skill: Even or odd number of shapes - up to 20
skills.prompt: This topic covers Even or odd number of shapes - up to 20 for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.7(A)). Even numbers split into two equal groups; odd numbers have one leftover. Even numbers end in 0,2,4,6,8; odd in 1,3,5,7,9. Key concepts include recognizing from the ones digit, pairing objects, and addition patterns. Vocabulary: even, odd, pair, ones digit, pattern, remainder. Common misconceptions include checking the wrong digit and uncertainty about zero. Activities should include pairing, sorting, and pattern exploration appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content, help students see patterns and relationships in numbers and operations. Use visual models like bar diagrams, number lines, and tables to make abstract concepts concrete for 7-8-year-olds.

skills.skill: Add 10 or 100
skills.prompt: This topic covers Add 10 or 100 for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.7(B)). Students mentally add or subtract 10 or 100, developing place value fluency. Adding 10 changes the tens digit; adding 100 changes the hundreds digit. Vocabulary: add, subtract, ten, hundred, mental math, pattern, place value. Common misconceptions include changing the wrong digit and boundary crossing errors. Activities should include hundred chart work, mental math challenges, and pattern recognition appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content, help students see patterns and relationships in numbers and operations. Use visual models like bar diagrams, number lines, and tables to make abstract concepts concrete for 7-8-year-olds.

skills.prompt: This topic covers Subtract 10 or 100 for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.7(B)). Students mentally add or subtract 10 or 100, developing place value fluency. When creating content, help students see patterns and relationships in numbers and operations. Use visual models like bar diagrams, number lines, and tables to make abstract concepts concrete for 7-8-year-olds.

skills.skill: Input/output tables - add and subtract by 10
skills.prompt: This topic covers Input/output tables - add and subtract by 10 for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.7(B)). Input/output tables show number pairs related by a consistent rule. Students identify rules and find missing values. Key concepts include consistent operation application, analyzing multiple pairs, and extending patterns. Vocabulary: input, output, rule, pattern, function, table, operation. Common misconceptions include inconsistent rule application and confusing direction. Activities should include completing tables, finding rules, and creating original tables appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content, help students see patterns and relationships in numbers and operations. Use visual models like bar diagrams, number lines, and tables to make abstract concepts concrete for 7-8-year-olds.

skills.skill: Addition word problems - sums to 20
skills.prompt: This topic covers Addition word problems - sums to 20 for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.7(C)). Students solve addition word problems, identifying joining/combining situations, writing equations, and computing sums. Vocabulary: add, sum, total, altogether, equation, model. Common misconceptions include choosing the wrong operation and not checking reasonableness. Activities should include modeling, equation writing, and varied contexts appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content, help students see patterns and relationships in numbers and operations. Use visual models like bar diagrams, number lines, and tables to make abstract concepts concrete for 7-8-year-olds. Encourage flexible thinking by asking students to solve problems in multiple ways and explain their reasoning. Build connections between different operations and representations.

skills.skill: Subtraction word problems - up to 20
skills.prompt: This topic covers Subtraction word problems - up to 20 for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.7(C)). Students solve subtraction word problems, identifying take-away, comparison, and missing-part situations. Vocabulary: subtract, difference, remaining, fewer, how many more. skills.skill: Write the addition sentence - up to two digits
skills.prompt: This topic covers Write the addition sentence - up to two digits for Grade 2 students (TEKS 2.7(C)). Students translate word problems into equations. Key concepts include identifying quantities and operations, writing with correct structure, and solving. Vocabulary: equation, sentence, write, represent, addend, sum, difference. Common misconceptions include wrong number order (for subtraction) and wrong operation. Activities should include guided equation writing and matching exercises appropriate for ages 7-8. When creating content, help students see patterns and relationships in numbers and operations.
